titleStmt.title: Letter from Dorothy Duffin to her mother Maria Duffin, 8 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Dorothy Duffin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This is a letter from Dorothy Duffin (b. 1881) to her mother Maria Duffin (1854-1954).
This letter is part of a rich correspondence between various members of the Duffin family, a large prosperous family living in Strandtown, Belfast. Several family members served in the war, including Major Terence Duffin, who served as a staff officer with 107 Brigade, and later with Royal Irish Rifles; Major Charles G Duffin MC, Royal Field Artillery; and their sisters Emma, Celia and Dorothy who served as Voluntary Aid Detachments (VADS) in Egypt and France, and with the YMCA.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

correspAction.placeName: No 16 General Hospital, Le Tréport, France

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
On Active Service
WITH THE BRITISH EXPEDITIONARY FORCE THE BRITISH EXPEDITIONARY FORCE,
c/o General Post Office,
LONDON. B 16 Gen: Hospital Nov 8th 1915 My Dear Mamma. I hope to hear from you
to-morrow. I am awfully happy & have been
having a splendid time & I am very free now
owing to Mrs Bell being here. You see my
choice with the cross on him but I hope to
send you a better one soon. The Colonel
is delighted with us and says we are
made for each other - he called to see me and I
returned it Saturday. Everyone is pleased
about it as Lester is awfully popular here
with everyone, of course it is hopeless to keep
it a secret here as I go about with him
everywhere. We had a busy day in Dieppe
as Capt. Worthian was motoring over and took us with
him & yesterday we had another
long day in the town as the Colonel gave
us his car for the day. Work here
has been so slack that we have been seeing
each other a very great deal but an away is
expected tomorrow which will probably make a
difference. There is no use making plans about
coming home then til I hear from you. He
expects to get leave time soon & if so will
come back with me. I must stop & get to work now. Much love
to all Dear. Do hope you'll like what you can
see of his ugly, old face. He is Dutch,
English & Spanish. He is so demented with
me I don't know how he'll ever let me go! Your loving Dorothy Colonel Thomson is the most charming man, he has been awfully kind to us and says he
feels responsible for the whole thing but he
properly certain we've done the right thing.
